author: Rahil Pirani
description: Sync your Obsidian notes to your self-hosted Second Brain MCP server on Cloudflare Workers. Search your brain from inside Obsidian. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Rahil Pirani
manifest.description: Sync your notes to your self-hosted Second Brain MCP server on Cloudflare Workers.
manifest.name: Second Brain Sync

name: Second Brain Sync

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.2.0

This release ships two new features: a persistent search sidebar and the ability to save any recall result directly as an Obsidian note.

---

### Search sidebar (#2)

The old modal-based memory search has been replaced with a **persistent sidebar panel** that stays open while you work.

- Open it via the new ribbon icon or the **Search memories** command
- Results show title, relevance score, tags, and an AI-generated insight callout
- Click any result to expand it inline and read the full memory content; click again to collapse
- Handles loading states, errors, and empty results gracefully

---

### Save recall results as notes (#3)

You can now **materialize any search result as a vault note** — closing the loop between recalled memories and your local knowledge base.

**Three ways to save:**
- **Save all as note** — a button above the results list creates a combined Markdown note containing every result, with frontmatter tagging the original query and timestamp
- **Save individual result** — a small save icon on each row creates a standalone note from that single memory
- **Save insight** — a save icon on the AI insight callout creates a note from the generated insight (tagged `type: insight` in frontmatter)

All three open a small confirmation modal where you can edit the note title and destination folder before saving. The folder defaults to your configured Import Folder setting.

**Notes include:**
- YAML frontmatter: `query`, `recalled_at`, `source: second-brain` (insight notes also get `type: insight`)
- Memory content cleaned up with structural Markdown normalization (consistent heading/list spacing, bullet style, code-fence preservation)
- Duplicate filenames handled automatically with `(1)`, `(2)` suffixes — no overwrites

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 1.1.2 — Scanner compliance fixes

Resolves errors and warnings flagged by the Obsidian community plugin scanner:

- **Settings re-render**: extracted a private `render()` method so `display()` is never called internally — eliminates disallowed eslint-disable comments
- **API compatibility**: removed `setDestructive()` which is not available in the declared minimum app version
- **Promise handling**: settings async callbacks and setTimeout handler no longer return promises in void contexts
- **Type safety**: tightened `processFrontMatter`, `JSON.parse`, and `loadData()` call sites — no more unsafe `any` assignments

No behavior changes — all fixes are under the hood.

---

## 1.1.1 — Quality & compatibility fixes

Resolves warnings flagged during Obsidian community plugin review:

- **Type safety**: eliminated unsafe `any` throughout API response handling and memory import flow
- **Vault privacy**: import deduplication now uses an in-memory cache instead of scanning every file in your vault
- **Deprecated APIs**: replaced `setWarning()` with `setDestructive()` on the reset cache button
- **Promise handling**: ribbon icon and editor command callbacks now properly handle async operations
- **Timer APIs**: switched to `window.setTimeout` / `window.clearTimeout` for full Obsidian compatibility

No behavior changes — all fixes are under the hood.

---

## Your Second Brain is now bidirectional

Until now the plugin only pushed notes *out* to your Second Brain. With 1.1.0, memories flow back in — anything stored in your Second Brain (from any AI tool) can be pulled into your Obsidian vault as a proper Markdown note.

---

## ✦ Import memories into Obsidian

Run **Import memories** from the command palette and the plugin fetches memories from your Second Brain and drops them into a folder of your choice as Markdown files — complete with frontmatter metadata, source, and timestamps.

**How it works:**
- Tag memories in your Second Brain with any tag you choose (e.g. `obsidian-inbox`)
- Set that tag in **Settings → Import behavior → Import tag**
- Run the import command — new memories land in your vault instantly
- Already-imported memories are skipped automatically on every subsequent run
- Optional: enable **Pull on startup** to import silently every time Obsidian opens

This means memories captured by AI tools outside Obsidian — meeting notes, decisions, ideas — can flow back into your vault without any manual copy-paste.

---

## Bug fixes & improvements (1.1.0)

- **Auto-sync now works without restarting Obsidian** — toggling it in settings takes effect immediately
- **Re-sync no longer duplicates content** — previously, saving a synced note appended the full content again on every save. Now it replaces cleanly
- **Multi-chunk notes update correctly** — all chunk IDs are tracked in frontmatter so every chunk gets updated on re-sync, not just the first
- **Inline tags work for sync** — `#brain` in the note body now triggers sync, not just frontmatter tags
- **Rename handling** — renaming a tagged note automatically re-syncs it so the title stays current in your Second Brain
- **Sync timestamp in properties** — each note now shows a `second-brain-synced` field with the last sync date and time
- **Status bar lifecycle fixed** — toggling the status bar off now properly removes the element

---

## Contributors

Huge thanks to **@ThWinchester** for building the import feature that made 1.1.0 possible.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/rahilp/second-brain-obsidian-plugin/compare/1.1.1...1.1.2

releases.author: rahilp
releases.description:
# Second Brain Sync — v1.0.3

Focused update improving sync reliability and performance. This version removes the search UI in favor of using your Second Brain through MCP-compatible AI tools, while adding append functionality to update existing notes instead of creating duplicates.

## What's New

### Append Functionality
- **Update existing notes** — Notes now append updates to existing entries instead of creating duplicates
- **Automatic tracking** — Plugin stores a `second-brain-id` in your note's frontmatter to track synced entries
- **First sync creates, subsequent syncs append** — Initial sync creates a new entry, all future syncs append updates with timestamps

### Improved Auto-Sync
- **Configurable delay** — Set how long to wait after you stop typing before syncing (3-30 seconds, default 5s)
- **No more spam** — Auto-sync now waits for you to pause typing instead of syncing after every few words
- **Duplicate prevention** — Fixed issue where notes would sync twice when frontmatter was modified

### Streamlined Experience
- **Removed search UI** — Search/recall functionality removed from plugin (use your Second Brain through Claude, ChatGPT, or other MCP-compatible tools)
- **Cleaner interface** — Removed sidebar panel, search ribbon icon, and search command
- **Focus on sync** — Plugin now focuses entirely on reliable note syncing

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed duplicate saves when frontmatter is modified during sync
- Improved error handling with better JSON response parsing
- Better connection error messages

## Breaking Changes
- Search/recall functionality has been removed from the plugin
- To search your Second Brain, use MCP-compatible AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.)

## Requirements
- Obsidian v1.7.2 or later
- A deployed Second Brain MCP server on Cloudflare Workers with the `/append` endpoint (v1.1.0+)

## Upgrade Notes
If you're upgrading from v1.0.0-1.0.2:
1. The search sidebar and commands are no longer available
2. Existing notes will automatically use append functionality on next sync
3. Configure auto-sync delay in Settings → Second Brain Sync if needed

## Features (Unchanged)
- Sync tagged notes or your entire vault
- Auto-sync on save with configurable delay
- Chunking for long notes (1,600+ characters)
- Status bar showing last sync time
- Test connection button

releases.author: rahilp
releases.description:
## Second Brain Sync — v1.0.0

Sync your Obsidian notes to your self-hosted Second Brain MCP server on Cloudflare Workers. Recall anything from inside Obsidian or across Claude, ChatGPT, and any MCP-compatible AI tool.

### Features

- **Sync tagged notes or your entire vault** — choose between syncing only notes with a specific frontmatter tag or all notes in your vault
- **Auto-sync on save** — tagged or all notes sync automatically whenever you save
- **Chunking for long notes** — notes over 1,600 characters are split into overlapping segments so each section gets a clean embedding and surfaces precisely in search
- **Sidebar search panel** — search your second brain from inside Obsidian without leaving your vault
- **Status bar** — shows last sync time at a glance
- **Test connection** — verify your Worker URL and token are correct before syncing

### Requirements

- Obsidian v1.7.2 or later
- A deployed [Second Brain MCP server](https://github.com/rahilp/second-brain-cloudflare) on Cloudflare Workers

### Installation

Install directly from Obsidian Community Plugins by searching **Second Brain Sync**, or install manually by downloading `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release and copying them to `.obsidian/plugins/second-brain-sync/` in your vault.

### Setup

1. Enable the plugin in Settings → Community Plugins
2. Go to Settings → Second Brain Sync
3. Enter your Worker URL and Auth token
4. Click **Test** to verify the connection
5. Set your sync mode and hit **Sync all**
